title: halimium ocymoides var. algarvense
intro: Halimium ocymoides var. algarvense, also known as rock rose, is a subspecies of the rock rose plant, a type of flowering succulent native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Halimium ocymoides var. algarvense include soothing and calming effects on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potential antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
